reasoning: The government of Mali, led by a military junta under Gen Assimi Goïta, issued a presidential decree on May 13, 2025, completely dissolving all political parties and political organizations. This structural move followed "national consultations" in April where it was recommended that Goïta's rule be extended for another five years (until 2030). Given that this decree was just formalized weeks before the July 1, 2025, resolution date, the timeline for a reversal is extremely short. Furthermore, the junta has deeply entrenched itself against external pressure by withdrawing from the regional bloc ECOWAS, which previously applied sanctions to force democratic transitions. With political opposition leaders being arrested and civil space systematically closed, there is virtually no domestic or international mechanism likely to force a full or partial reversal in the next ~45 days. The probability is set to the floor value of 0.02.
